content: We can frame LLM self-reflection mechanisms as a formalization of the linguistic concept of reflection as a metacognitive learning tool, drawing on the Reflexion agent framework to turn the passive lexical sense of "reflection" (meaning reflecting on thought) into an active, iterative dynamic memory process for large language models. This creates a bidirectional link: the common linguistic definition of reflexion gives us a intuitive blueprint for designing more human-aligned LLM self-reflection, rather than only building reflection mechanisms from purely technical error-correction objectives.
